---
title: "ENVS 193DS Infographic"
date: "June 6, 2026" 
author: "Danielle Sur" 
format: pdf 
editor: source
---

# Set up

```{r}
#| message: false

# loaded in packages
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(here)
library(webr)
library(ggimage)

# read in data and saved as object called `rxntime`
rxntime <- read_csv("data/rxn-time.csv")
# read in data and saved as object called `pie`
pie <- read_csv("data/pie.csv")
```

Data last updated on May 29, 2026.

# Part 3. Visualizing data

```{r}
my_cols <- c(
  biking = "slateblue",
  volleyball = "dodgerblue",
  walking = "lightskyblue"
)
```

## Pie chart

### Cleaning data

```{r}
# created a new object from pie
pie_clean <- pie |>
  # cleaned column names
  clean_names() |> 
  # selected columns of interest
  select(exercise_type, social_interaction, count) |> 
  # changed existing abbreviations
  mutate(social_interaction = case_when(
    social_interaction == "y" ~ "Yes",
    social_interaction == "n" ~ "No"
  )) |> 
  # removed missing values
  drop_na()
```

### Visualization

```{r}
piee <- as.data.frame(pie_clean); piee

# created a pie donut chart using the webr package and pie_clean df
PieDonut(data = pie_clean,
         # pie slices by exercise type, outlined by social interaction
         aes(exercise_type, social_interaction, count = count),
         # outer circle of the pie adds up to 100%
         r0 = 0.5, # inner ring thickness
         r1 = 1, # outer ring thickness
         ratioByGroup = FALSE,
         title = "Social Interaction Percentage by Exercise Type")
```

## Box / jitter plot

### Cleaning data

```{r}
# created a new object from rxntime
rxntime_clean <- rxntime |>
  # cleaned column names
  clean_names() |> 
  # selected columns of interest
  select(exercise_type, reaction_time_avg_ms) |> 
  # changed existing abbreviations
  mutate(exercise_type = case_when(
    exercise_type == "volleyball" ~ "Volleyball",
    exercise_type == "biking" ~ "Biking",
    exercise_type == "walking" ~ "Walking"
  )) |> 
  # removed missing values
  drop_na()
```

### Visualization

```{r}
# base layer: ggplot
ggplot(data = rxntime_clean,
       # x-axis: exercise type
       # y-axis: mean reaction time
       # color by exercise type
       mapping = aes(x = exercise_type,
                     y = reaction_time_avg_ms,
                     color = exercise_type)) +
  # first layer: boxplot
  geom_boxplot() +
  # second layer: jitter for raw data
  geom_jitter(height = 0) +
  # relabeled x- and y-axes
  labs(x = "Exercise Type",
       y = "Mean Reaction Time (ms)") +
  # removed legend
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  # custom colors
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Biking" = "slateblue",
                                "Volleyball" = "dodgerblue",
                                "Walking" = "lightskyblue")) +
  # removed grid lines
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.line = element_line(color = "black"))
```

^^ add drawing to negative space in the plot

## Timeseries plot

```{r}
# base layer: ggplot
ggplot(data = rxntime_clean,
       # x-axis: date
       # y-axis: rxntime
       # color, line type, and size by region
       mapping = aes(x = date_mm_dd_yyyy,
                     y = reaction_time_avg_ms,
                     color = exercise_type,
                     linetype = exercise_type,
                     linewidth = exercise_type)) +
  # adding lines
  geom_line() + 
  # relabeling axes
  # adding title and subtitle
  labs( x = "Date",
        y = "Mean Reaction Time (ms)",
        color = "exercise_type",
        title = "Mean Reaction Times by Exercise Type") +
  # custom colors
  scale_color_manual(values =
                       c("Biking" = "red",
                         "Volleyball" = "green4")) +
  # custom line type
  scale_linetype_manual(values =
                          c("Biking" = "dotted",
                            "Volleyball" = "solid")) +
  # custom line sizes
  scale_linewidth_manual(values = 
                      c("Biking" = 1,
                        "Volleyball" = 0.7)) +
  # adjusting figure presentation
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45), # angling x-axis text
    axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0, # repositioning y-axis title
                                vjust = 0.6, # y-axis title placement
                                size = 8, # y-axis title size
                                margin = margin(t = 20,
                                                r = 0,
                                                b = 0,
                                                l = 0)),
    # x-axis title size
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 8),
    # plot title adjustments
    plot.title.position = "plot",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0, # shifting plot title to the left
                              size = 10), # changing plot title size
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 8), # changing plot subtitle size
    legend.position = c(1, 1), # legend to the top right
    legend.justification = c(1, 1), # justifying legend
    panel.background = element_blank(), # removing panel background
    axis.line.y = element_blank(), # no y-axis line
    panel.grid = element_blank(), # removing panel grid
    axis.ticks = element_blank(), # removing axis ticks
    legend.title = element_blank() # removing the legend title
  )
```

# Part 4. Write about your process

A classmate on Friday used birds to represent raw data points.
